essence: Dark Factory must gain deep understanding of KyberAgent and the Kyber Extension SDK so it can control the agent via MCP/API for direct deployment of built extensions into the local harness and integration testing over its communication channels, with escalated permissions and a dedicated ever-expanding document to track what control surfaces Dark Factory needs.

actions.text: Start and maintain a document mapping what control surfaces (MCP/API, permissions, deployment, comms channels) Dark Factory needs over KyberAgent

essence: The combined Plaud+OMI viewer is officially named Captain's Log and will be hosted as a KyberAgent extension so it can reach an LLM through the daemon for tagging/ontology without building its own API layer, with a configurable pulse that polls Plaud and OMI within a daily window roughly every 10 minutes.

actions.text: Host the Captain's Log viewer as a KyberAgent extension with LLM access via the daemon
Make the Captain's Log pulse configurable (daily window, ~10-minute cadence) through the extension system

essence: Governance critique of yesterday's Dark Factory session: changes to factory-running code should be blocked from running through the factory itself, the unapproved 'war room' namespace displaced the existing lanes/stations naming, the main conversation window lacks a chaperone/observer, task-launch has fragmented into three inconsistent paths (ad-hoc claude, orchestrator CLI, and the bypassed Switchboard/WatchTower web harness), and there is no clean way to bootstrap a new context-rich main conversation.

actions.text: Decide policy: block/flag tickets that modify factory-running code instead of executing them through the factory
Review and unwind the unapproved 'war room' namespace back to factory lanes/stations naming
Add a chaperone/observer over the main factory conversation window
Consolidate task launching onto the orchestrator/Switchboard-WatchTower path instead of ad-hoc claude invocations

essence: Consultation with Linda (Challenge DV): instead of a $25k one-off video production quote, David demoed AI-generated content (Hyperframes auto-edited videos from Joy's raw one-minute clips, HeyGen avatar, auto-generated checklists/PDFs in brand colours) and pitched an ongoing engagement of weekly 90-minute coaching sessions plus a monthly retainer with bespoke micro-tools, pushing Claude subscriptions over Copilot; Linda is keen and awaits pricing options.

actions.text: Send Linda a set of engagement/pricing options (weekly 90-min sessions + monthly retainer + tool-building)

essence: David articulates the correct Kyber extension architecture using AngelEye as the model: a separately-running web server/daemon reads the file system and serves the client page, the client renders in an iframe inside the host app with socket communication, and the iframe must never talk to the backend through the host — pushing back on a muddled model being presented to him.

Doc-review session on the extension repo: refresh-context/system-context docs aren't designed for repos David manages that already have good docs and KDDs, he approves the agent's three recommended changes and removal of test-only screenshots, and questions the panel-vs-mount terminology given extensions are deliberately host-disconnected iframes.

actions.text: Apply the approved doc plan and remove test-only screenshots from the repository

essence: In-person exchange where David explains KDD's two librarians (Lisa for lifecycle learnings, Lexi for reference) and the promotion ladder — issues logged per unit of work, promoted to a pattern with real code examples after three recurrences, feeding a post-plan verification step against repo history — while the other party maps it to smart-contract-style rubrics (security/stability/simplicity/speed) and agent staking/rating marketplaces.

Casual conversation touching on Anthropic's 'unknown unknowns' agent pattern (broad north stars outperform prescriptive plans), David's ticket-automation work, and an unresolved closing question about how smart contracts fit a dual-loop planning approach.

Working session with Angela: create a private achieve-mvp repo under the Supporting Potential org with Angela's access, invoice $1000 and attach her card to a Claude Max plan, set the North Star of upgrading Angela from consultant to technology/AI implementation partner, target a 3-week visual interactive demo (not a full app) for Achieve (~$150M NDIS provider), build her a content engine of 100 one-minute NDIS scripts, and redeploy the SupportSignal app on fresh Vercel+Supabase as a live demo asset.

actions.text: Create private achieve-mvp repo under Supporting Potential org and grant Angela write access
Angela to load her Achieve mapping/workflow outputs into the repo for Claude context-load
Angela to generate 100 one-minute NDIS scripts via Claude Code and record them for daily video release
Redeploy the SupportSignal application on a new Vercel + Supabase instance as a working demo
Invoice Angela $1000 and set up Claude Max plan billing on her card

essence: David directs the agent working on his frame-extension changes to merge to his branch and run option-2 fully-closed end-to-end tests alongside UAT, and queries an earlier claim that ~10 tests were failing (suspecting integration rather than unit tests).

actions.text: Merge to David's branch and run option-2 end-to-end tests for the frame extension changes

essence: David pushes back on customizing Lisa for one repository's unique issues — Lisa's scope is the KDD folder only, not a repo's general documentation folder or application — and asks what has gone wrong in their thinking before merging documentation-only work.

actions.text: Keep Lisa scoped to the KDD folder; do not customize her for per-repo documentation quirks
